title: Smoky Juicy Turkish Chicken Kebabs
description: Kidfriendly, halalfriendly Turkish chicken kebabs with a sweet treacle glaze and a smoky flavor, perfect for a quick dinner.
instructions: Preheat the grill or grill pan over medium‑high heat. If using a charcoal grill, light the coals until they are covered with gray ash to give a natural smoke flavor.
Whisk together olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, pepper, minced garlic, lemon juice, and treacle until well combined.

Pat the chicken thighs dry with paper towels and cut them into 2‑inch bite‑sized cubes. Add the chicken to the bowl and toss until each piece is coated in the spice‑treacle mixture. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (up to 2 hours) to marinate.
Thread the marinated chicken cubes onto the soaked skewers, about 4–5 pieces per skewer. Shake off excess sauce.
Place the skewers on the preheated grill. Cook for 3–4 minutes on each side, turning occasionally and brushing with a little extra treacle or oil to keep them moist.
Use a meat thermometer to confirm the internal temperature has reached 74 °C (165 °F). The kebabs should be golden‑brown and slightly charred.
Remove the skewers from the grill and let them rest for 3 minutes to allow juices to redistribute, making them extra juicy and kid‑friendly.
Serve immediately with flatbread, a small side of cucumber yogurt dip, or a light salad. Garnish with fresh parsley if desired.
